description: iOS app that empowers fitness enthusiasts to create beautiful, customizable images of their workouts for sharing on social media. Imports workouts from Apple Health and lets users choose which statistics to display, how to style them, and what background to use.

userStories.title: Initialize Xcode project with SwiftUI and SwiftData
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a properly configured Xcode project so that I have a foundation to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: Xcode project created with SwiftUI lifecycle
SwiftData configured as persistence layer
MVVM folder structure established (Models, Views, ViewModels, Services)
Swift Testing target configured for unit tests
XCUITest target configured for UI tests
Project builds and runs on iOS 17+ simulator
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up HealthKit entitlements and permissions
userStories.description: As a developer, I need HealthKit properly configured so the app can request workout data access.
acceptanceCriteria: HealthKit capability added to project
Info.plist contains NSHealthShareUsageDescription
HealthService protocol defined with methods: requestAuthorization(), isAuthorized()
Mock HealthService created for testing that returns configurable results
Unit tests pass for permission request flow
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up Photos framework entitlements
userStories.description: As a developer, I need photo library access configured so users can select background images.
acceptanceCriteria: Photo Library capability added to project
Info.plist contains NSPhotoLibraryUsageDescription
PhotoService protocol defined with methods: requestAuthorization(), fetchLastPhoto(), pickPhoto()
Mock PhotoService created for testing
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create Workout data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Workout model to store imported workout data locally.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftData @Model class Workout with properties: id (UUID), type (WorkoutType), startDate (Date), endDate (Date), distance (Double?), duration (TimeInterval), route ([Coordinate]?), calories (Double?), avgHeartRate (Double?), maxHeartRate (Double?), avgPace (Double?), elevationGain (Double?)
WorkoutType enum with cases: running, cycling, swimming, hiking, walking, other
Coordinate struct with latitude and longitude properties
Unit tests verify model initialization
Unit tests verify model persistence with SwiftData
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create ShareConfiguration data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a model to persist user's sharing preferences and styling choices.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftData @Model class ShareConfiguration with properties: selectedStats ([StatisticType]), backgroundType (BackgroundType), fontName (String), fontSize (CGFloat), textColor (String - hex), textPosition (TextPosition), selectedPhotoData (Data?)
BackgroundType enum with cases: routeMap, lastPhoto, selectedPhoto
TextPosition enum with cases: topLeft, topRight, bottomLeft, bottomRight, center
Optional relationship to Workout (one config per workout)
Unit tests verify model persistence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create StatisticType enum and display logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a way to represent available statistics and format them for display.
acceptanceCriteria: StatisticType enum with cases: distance, duration, pace, startTime, calories, avgHeartRate, maxHeartRate, elevationGain
Each case has displayName computed property (e.g., 'Distance', 'Avg Heart Rate')
Each case has unit computed property (e.g., 'mi', 'bpm', 'ft')
StatFormatter struct with static methods to format each stat type
Distance formatted as 'X.XX mi'
Duration formatted as 'H:MM:SS' or 'MM:SS'
Pace formatted as 'X:XX /mi'
Heart rate formatted as 'XXX bpm'
Unit tests cover all formatting including edge cases (zero, nil, large values)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Implement HealthService workout fetch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see my recent workouts so I can choose one to share.
acceptanceCriteria: HealthService has fetchWorkouts(from: Date, to: Date) async throws -> [Workout] method
Fetches workouts from HealthKit for last 30 days by default
Filters to GPS-based cardio types: running, cycling, swimming, hiking, walking
Converts HKWorkout to app's Workout model correctly
Returns empty array (not error) when no workouts found
Throws HealthServiceError.permissionDenied when not authorized
Unit tests with MockHealthStore verify fetch logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fetch workout route data from HealthKit
userStories.description: As a user, I want my workout route included so I can display it as a map background.
acceptanceCriteria: HealthService has fetchRoute(for workout: HKWorkout) async throws -> [Coordinate]? method
Extracts CLLocation array from HKWorkoutRoute
Converts CLLocation to app's Coordinate model
Returns nil for workouts without route data (treadmill, pool)
Stores route coordinates in Workout model during fetch
Unit tests verify route extraction with mock data
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fetch detailed workout statistics from HealthKit
userStories.description: As a user, I want detailed stats like heart rate and elevation so I can display them.
acceptanceCriteria: HealthService fetches average heart rate for workout time range
HealthService fetches max heart rate for workout time range
HealthService fetches elevation gain from workout metadata
Calculates average pace from distance/duration
Missing data stored as nil in Workout model (not zero)
Unit tests verify stat calculations with known inputs
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create WorkoutListViewModel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ViewModel to manage workout list state and business logic.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkoutListViewModel as @Observable class
Properties: workouts ([Workout]), isLoading (Bool), errorMessage (String?), selectedFilter (WorkoutType?)
fetchWorkouts() method calls HealthService and updates state
isLoading true during fetch, false after completion
errorMessage populated on failure, nil on success
Unit tests verify all state transitions: loading, success, error
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create workout list view
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a list of my recent workouts so I can select one to customize and share.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkoutListView with SwiftUI List displaying workouts
Sorted by date with newest first
Each row shows: workout type SF Symbol icon, formatted date, distance, duration
Pull-to-refresh triggers fetchWorkouts()
Empty state view when workouts array is empty
Loading spinner (ProgressView) when isLoading is true
Error alert when errorMessage is not nil
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: list displays, pull-to-refresh works

userStories.title: Implement workout type filtering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter workouts by type so I can find a specific activity quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Horizontal ScrollView of filter chips above workout list
Filter options: All, Running, Cycling, Swimming, Hiking, Walking
Tapping chip updates selectedFilter in ViewModel
List filters to show only matching workout types
All chip shows unfiltered list
Selected chip has distinct visual style (filled vs outlined)
Empty state shows 'No [type] workouts found' when filter yields no results
Unit tests verify filtering logic in ViewModel
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: filters work correctly

userStories.title: Create ShareEditorViewModel
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a ViewModel to manage editor state and image generation.
acceptanceCriteria: ShareEditorViewModel as @Observable class
Initialized with Workout parameter
Properties: workout (Workout), configuration (ShareConfiguration), previewImage (UIImage?)
Default configuration created if none exists for workout
Methods: updateSelectedStats(), updateBackgroundType(), updateFont(), updateFontSize(), updateTextColor(), updateTextPosition()
Configuration auto-saved to SwiftData on changes
Unit tests verify state management and persistence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create share editor view skeleton
userStories.description: As a user, I want an editor screen where I can customize my workout image.
acceptanceCriteria: Navigation from WorkoutListView to ShareEditorView on row tap
Selected workout passed to ShareEditorView
Preview area (Image view) in upper portion showing placeholder
Scrollable customization area in lower portion with section headers
Share button in navigation bar (non-functional for now)
Back navigation returns to workout list
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: navigation works, layout displays correctly

userStories.title: Implement stat selection interface
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose which statistics appear on my image.
acceptanceCriteria: Stats section in editor with multi-select list
Each row shows: checkbox, stat name, current value from workout
Stats unavailable for this workout (nil values) are grayed and not selectable
Maximum 6 stats can be selected - additional taps show alert
Minimum 1 stat required - cannot deselect last stat
Selection updates configuration.selectedStats immediately
Unit tests verify selection logic and constraints
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: selection works, constraints enforced

userStories.title: Implement background selection interface
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose my image background from three options.
acceptanceCriteria: Background section with Picker (segmented style): Map, Last Photo, Choose Photo
Map option disabled with explanation if workout has no route
Selecting Last Photo calls PhotoService.fetchLastPhoto()
Selecting Choose Photo presents PHPickerViewController
Selected photo stored in configuration.selectedPhotoData
Selection updates configuration.backgroundType immediately
Unit tests verify background switching logic
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: picker works, photo selection works

userStories.title: Implement text styling controls
userStories.description: As a user, I want to customize how the statistics text looks.
acceptanceCriteria: Style section with font picker (Picker with .menu style)
Font options: SF Pro, Helvetica Neue, Avenir, Georgia, Menlo (5 fonts)
Font size slider with range 12-32, shows current value
Color picker using ColorPicker for text color
Position picker with 5 options: Top Left, Top Right, Bottom Left, Bottom Right, Center
All changes update configuration immediately
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: all controls work and update configuration

userStories.title: Create route map renderer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to render the workout route as a styled map image.
acceptanceCriteria: MapRenderer service with renderRouteMap(coordinates: [Coordinate], size: CGSize) async -> UIImage? method
Uses MKMapSnapshotter to capture map image
Draws route as polyline overlay on snapshot
Route line: 4pt width, accent color, rounded caps
Map region auto-fits route with padding
Returns nil if coordinates are empty
Supports both 1080x1080 and 1080x1920 sizes
Unit tests verify method doesn't crash with valid/empty input
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create statistics overlay renderer
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to render selected statistics as text overlay on the image.
acceptanceCriteria: OverlayRenderer service with renderStatsOverlay(stats: [(StatisticType, String)], config: ShareConfiguration, size: CGSize) -> UIImage method
Renders stats as formatted text block using configured font, size, color
Positions text according to config.textPosition with 20pt padding from edges
Adds 2pt dark shadow offset (1,1) for readability on light backgrounds
Handles 1-6 stats stacked vertically
Each stat shows: icon, name, value (e.g., '🏃 Distance: 5.2 mi')
Unit tests verify text positioning for each TextPosition case
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create composite image generator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to combine background and overlay into final shareable image.
acceptanceCriteria: ImageGenerator service with generateImage(workout: Workout, config: ShareConfiguration, format: ImageFormat) async -> UIImage? method
ImageFormat enum: square (1080x1080), story (1080x1920)
Composites background image with stats overlay using Core Graphics
For routeMap: uses MapRenderer output as background
For lastPhoto/selectedPhoto: scales and crops photo to fill
Adds subtle 'ShareMyRun' watermark in bottom corner (small, semi-transparent)
Returns nil if background cannot be generated
Unit tests verify composition logic for each background type
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Connect image preview to generator
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a live preview of my customized image as I make changes.
acceptanceCriteria: ShareEditorViewModel calls ImageGenerator when configuration changes
Debounce updates by 0.3 seconds to avoid excessive regeneration
previewImage property updated with generated image
Preview area in ShareEditorView displays previewImage
Loading indicator shown while image generates
Preview aspect ratio matches selected format
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: preview updates when changing settings

userStories.title: Implement share sheet integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to share my customized image to any app via the system share sheet.
acceptanceCriteria: Share button in navigation bar triggers share flow
Generates final image at full resolution before sharing
Presents UIActivityViewController with generated UIImage
Includes text caption: '[WorkoutType] - [Distance] in [Duration]'
Loading indicator while generating final image
Handles share completion callback (dismisses sheet)
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: share sheet appears with image

userStories.title: Save image to camera roll option
userStories.description: As a user, I want to save my image to my camera roll as an alternative to sharing.
acceptanceCriteria: Save to Photos button in editor (below preview or in toolbar)
Requests photo library add-only permission if not granted
Generates final image and saves via PHPhotoLibrary
Shows success toast/alert: 'Image saved to Photos'
Shows error alert if save fails with reason
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: save button works, permission requested if needed

userStories.title: Create subscription/Pro tier data model
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a model to track Pro subscription status for future use.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftData @Model class UserSubscription with: isPro (Bool), expirationDate (Date?)
SubscriptionService protocol with isPro() -> Bool method
MockSubscriptionService for testing returns configurable value
Default implementation returns false (free tier) - hardcoded for MVP
Unit tests verify subscription check logic
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create automatic sharing preferences model (stubbed)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a stubbed data model for automatic sharing configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: SwiftData @Model class AutoShareConfig with: isEnabled (Bool), destinations ([String]), defaultTemplateId (UUID?)
All properties default to disabled/empty state
Model defined but not actively used in MVP
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add Settings screen with Pro section
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see app settings and what Pro features will offer.
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsView accessible from WorkoutListView (gear icon in toolbar)
Settings sections: General, Pro Features, About
Pro Features section shows 'Automatic Sharing' row with lock icon
Tapping locked row shows alert: 'Pro Feature - Coming Soon'
'Upgrade to Pro' button styled prominently (disabled in MVP)
About section shows app version
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: settings navigable, Pro section displays correctly

userStories.title: Implement onboarding flow
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a brief onboarding so I understand how to use the app.
acceptanceCriteria: OnboardingView with 3 pages using TabView with PageTabViewStyle
Page 1: Welcome - app name, icon, tagline
Page 2: HealthKit - explains workout import, shows permission request button
Page 3: Get Started - brief feature overview, 'Get Started' button
Page indicators at bottom
Skip button on pages 1-2
Completing onboarding sets UserDefaults.hasCompletedOnboarding = true
App shows onboarding only when hasCompletedOnboarding is false
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: onboarding flow works, only shows once

userStories.title: Handle HealthKit permission denied state
userStories.description: As a user who denied permissions, I want clear guidance on how to enable them.
acceptanceCriteria: WorkoutListView shows PermissionDeniedView when HealthService.isAuthorized() returns false
PermissionDeniedView explains why workouts aren't showing
'Open Settings' button opens UIApplication.openSettingsURLString
App re-checks permission when returning to foreground (scenePhase change)
Typecheck passes
Verify in simulator: permission denied state displays correctly

userStories.title: Implement error handling and user feedback
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback when something goes wrong.
acceptanceCriteria: AppError enum with cases: healthKitError, photoError, imageGenerationError, saveError
Each case has localizedDescription for user-friendly message
Toast component for transient success/error messages (auto-dismiss after 3s)
Alert used for errors requiring acknowledgment
Retry button shown for retryable errors (network, transient failures)
All async operations surface errors to UI (no silent failures)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add accessibility support
userStories.description: As a user with accessibility needs, I want to use the app with VoiceOver and Dynamic Type.
acceptanceCriteria: All buttons have .accessibilityLabel describing action
Workout rows have .accessibilityLabel with full workout summary
Images have .accessibilityLabel describing content
All text views support Dynamic Type (use .font(.body) etc, not fixed sizes)
Color contrast meets WCAG AA (4.5:1 for text)
Focus order follows logical reading order
Typecheck passes
Verify with Accessibility Inspector: no warnings
